REPLY FILED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBIT A [DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF JOHN SHANNON, DATED AUGUST 28, 2025], IN ITS ENTIRETY IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT ARGUES MR. SHANNON'S DECLARATION IS CONTRADICTED BY HIS EXTENSIVE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. THE COURT DISAGREES. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE IS NO DIRECT CONTRADICTION BETWEEN MR. SHANNON'S TESTIMONY AND HIS DECLARATION; INSTEAD, THE DECLARATION SUPPLEMENTS HIS TESTIMONY. (SEE HARRIS V. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING CO., INC. (2021) 68 CAL.APP.5TH 594, 601-607 [TRIAL COURT ERRED IN REFUSING TO GIVE ANY WEIGHT TO EXPERT DECLARATION REGARDING RE-ENTRAINMENT THEORY OF EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS]; TURLEY V. FAMILIAN CORP. (2017) 18 CAL.APP.5TH 969, 981-985 [D'AMICO RULE DID NOT APPLY WHERE THERE WAS "NO DIRECT CONTRADICTION" BETWEEN THE DECLARATION AND THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY.]) THE COURT DECLINES TO RULE ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS F AND G, AS THE COURT DID NOT RELY ON THE REFERENCED EVIDENCE. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY. REPORTER: TAMARA WILLAT CSR#4609 T: 415.272.1753 E: TAMARAWILLAT@SBCGLOBAL.NET (EPS/304).

MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 30, 2025, in Department 304, Line 1.
2)

Appearances: Kimberly Chu, ESQ. of Brayton Purcell LLP (via Zoom) for Plaintiff; Mirna Scheffy, ESQ. of Womble Bond and Dickinson for Defendant P.E. O'Hair & Co.

Court Reporter: Tamara Willat CSR#4609 T: 415.272.1753 E: tamarawillat@sbcglobal.net

Defendant P.E. O'Hair & Co. contests the court's tentative ruling.

Matter is argued and submitted, The court takes the matter under submission.

***After the hearing the court issues the following:

Defendant P.E. O'Hair & Co., erroneously sued as Westburne Supply, Inc.'s ("Defendant" or "P.E. O'Hair") Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Opposition filed. Reply filed.

Defendant's objections to Exhibit A [Declaration of Plaintiff John Shannon, dated August 28, 2025], in its entirety is OVERRULED. Defendant argues Mr. Shannon's declaration is contradicted by his extensive deposition testimony. The Court disagrees. The Court finds that there is no direct contradiction between Mr. Shannon's testimony and his declaration; instead, the declaration supplements his testimony. (See Harris v. Thomas Dee Engineering Co., Inc. (2021) 68 Cal.App.5th 594, 601-607 [trial court erred in refusing to give any weight to expert declaration regarding re-entrainment theory of exposure to asbestos]; Turley v. Familian Corp. (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 969, 981-985 [D'Amico rule did not apply where there was "no direct contradiction" between the declaration and the deposition testimony.])

The Court declines to rule on Defendant's objections to Exhibits F and G, as the Court did not rely on the referenced evidence.
